title: Kolaramma Temple
tagline: Kolaramma Temple — spiritual town of Karnataka

overview.short: Kolaramma is the presiding deity of the town of Kolar in Karnataka, India.
overview.description: Kolaramma is the presiding deity of the town of Kolar in Karnataka, India. The Kolaramma temple is thousand years old and built by the Thalakadu Gangas in the South Indian style. Later it was developed by the Cholas. The erstwhile maharajas of Mysore frequently visited this temple to get the blessings of Kolaramma.

topPlaces.name: Kolar, Karnataka
topPlaces.description: Kolar or Kolāra (Kannada: [koːlaːrɐ], historically "Kuvaḻālapura") is a city in the Indian state of Karnataka. It is the headquarters of Kolar district.

topPlaces.name: Kataripalya
topPlaces.description: Kataripalya is a place in the city of Kolar, Karnataka state of India. The name roughly translates to sword town in Kannada.

topPlaces.name: Therhalli
topPlaces.description: Thera Halli is a village in Karnataka, India. It is situated on top of the Antara Gange hill near Kolar.

topPlaces.name: Antara Gange
topPlaces.description: Antara Gange (also known as Anthargange) is a mountain situated in the Shathashrunga mountain range in the southeastern portion of the Indian state of Karnataka, Kolar district. Antara Gange literally means "Ganges from deep" in Kannada.

topPlaces.name: Kurudumale
topPlaces.description: Kurudumale is a village in the taluk of Mulbagal, Kolar district, Karnataka, India. It is located about 10 km from the mulubagal town, northerly.

topPlaces.name: Abbani
topPlaces.description: Abbani is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Kolar taluk of Kolar district in Karnataka.

items.note: Kolar or Kolāra (Kannada: [koːlaːrɐ], historically "Kuvaḻālapura") is a city in the Indian state of Karnataka. It is the headquarters of Kolar district.

Kurudumale is a village in the taluk of Mulbagal, Kolar district, Karnataka, India. It is located about 10 km from the mulubagal town, northerly.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kolaramma Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kolaramma Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kolaramma Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kempegowda International Airport, Bengaluru (47 km) and the nearest railway station is KSR Bengaluru City Junction (64 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kolaramma Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kolaramma Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kolaramma Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Kolaramma Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kolaramma Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kolar, Karnataka, Kataripalya, Therhalli, Antara Gange, Kurudumale.
